tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Create an Employer relation from person "John Connor" to organization "Cyberdyne Systems" with role "Engineer". List schemas first to find the relation typename.

content.text: Let me start by listing schemas to find the relation typename, and also search for the person and organization objects.
I'll start by listing the schemas and searching for both the person and organization objects in parallel.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

description.description: Description of the blueprint's purpose and functionality

source.description: Handlebars template source

instructions.description: Instructions that guide the AI assistant's behavior and responses

tools.description: Array of tools that the AI assistant can use when this blueprint is active

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Create a preson fullName="John Doe" with a reference to the organization "Cyberdyne Systems".

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Create a preson fullName="John Doe" with a reference to the organization "Cyberdyne Systems".

content.text: Let me first search for the organization "Cyberdyne Systems" and list schemas to find the correct types.
I'll start by searching for the organization "Cyberdyne Systems" and listing the available schemas to find the correct types.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

description.description: Description of the blueprint's purpose and functionality

source.description: Handlebars template source

instructions.description: Instructions that guide the AI assistant's behavior and responses

tools.description: Array of tools that the AI assistant can use when this blueprint is active

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

content.text: The organization "Cyberdyne Systems" doesn't exist yet. I'll need to create it first, and then create the person with a reference to it. Let me create the organization first.
The organization has been created. Now I'll create the person "John Doe" with a reference to the organization.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Delete the organization "Obsolete Corp".

content.text: Let me search for the organization "Obsolete Corp" first.
Let me find the organization "Obsolete Corp" first.
Found it. Now I'll delete the organization "Obsolete Corp".

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Update the organization "Old Name Inc" and change its name to "New Name Corp".

content.text: Let me search for the organization with the name "Old Name Inc" first.
Let me first find the organization "Old Name Inc" in the space.

result.description: Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database.
result.name: Database

content.text: I found the organization "Old Name Inc". Let me update its name to "New Name Corp".

response.delta: been successfully updated! The name has been

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Search for all organizations. How many are there?

content.text: Let me search for organizations by first checking the schemas to find the correct typename.
Let me search for organizations in the space. First, I'll check the schemas to find the correct typename for organizations.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

description.description: Description of the blueprint's purpose and functionality

source.description: Handlebars template source

instructions.description: Instructions that guide the AI assistant's behavior and responses

tools.description: Array of tools that the AI assistant can use when this blueprint is active

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

content.text: Now let me query for all organizations:

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Create an Employer relation from person "John Connor" to organization "Cyberdyne Systems" with role "Engineer". List schemas first to find the relation typename.

content.text: The user wants to create a relation between two objects. Let me first list schemas to find the relation typename, and also search for the person and organization objects.
I'll start by listing the schemas and searching for both "John Connor" and "Cyberdyne Systems" in parallel.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

description.description: Description of the blueprint's purpose and functionality

source.description: Handlebars template source

instructions.description: Instructions that guide the AI assistant's behavior and responses

tools.description: Array of tools that the AI assistant can use when this blueprint is active

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

content.text:
I found everything I need:

- **Schema**: `org.dxos.relation.employer` — a relation from a **Person** (source) to an **Organization** (target), with properties like `role`, `active`, `startDate`, `endDate`.
- **John Connor** (Person): `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTDR`
- **Cyberdyne Systems** (Organization): `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTDT`

Now I'll create the Employer relation.

response.delta: Employer relation** has been successfully created

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Add tag "important" to the organization "Tagged Corp".

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Remove tag "obsolete" from the organization "Untagged Corp".

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Add the organization "Context Corp" to the chat context.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

## Context Objects

  <dxn>dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTHJ</dxn>

</object>

response.delta: The user wants to remove an
organization from the chat context. Looking at

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>

List all available schemas. Tell me what typenames are available.

content.text: The user wants to see all available schemas and their typenames.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

description.description: Description of the blueprint's purpose and functionality

source.description: Handlebars template source

instructions.description: Instructions that guide the AI assistant's behavior and responses

tools.description: Array of tools that the AI assistant can use when this blueprint is active

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

response.delta:
are all the available schemas and their typenames:

| # | Typename | Name/Description |
Expando** — A flexible, schema-less object that accepts any additional properties | Record

|
| 2 | `org.dxos.relation.employer` | **Employer Relation** — Links a Person to an Organization (with

role, active status, start/end dates) | Relation |
| 3 | `org.dxos.type.blueprint` | **Blueprint** — Defines

AI assistant behavior with instructions, tools, and MCP servers | Record |
| 4
| `org.dxos.type.organization` | **Organization** — Represents a company/org with name, description, status, image

, website, and location | Record |
| 5 | `org.dxos.type.person` | **Person** — A contact/person

with full name, emails, phone numbers, addresses, job title, organization, and more | Record |
| 6 | `org.dxos.type.tag` | **Tag**
— A label that can be attached to any object (with optional hue/

): Expando, Blueprint, Organization, Person, and Tag
- **1 relation type**: Employer (connects

a Person → Organization)

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Search for all organizations. How many are there?

content.text: Let me search for organizations by first checking the schema list to find the correct typename for organizations.
Let me search for organizations in the space.

response.delta:

Would you like me to load
the full details for either or both of them?

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.text:
I found everything I need:

- **Relation schema**: `org.dxos.relation.employer` (source: Person → target: Organization, with a `role` field)
- **John Connor**: `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTDN`
- **Cyberdyne Systems**: `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTDQ`

Now I'll create the Employer relation.

response.delta:
**Employer** relation has been created successfully! Here's a summary:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Relation Type

** | `org.dxos.relation.employer` |
| **Source (Person)** | John Connor |
| **Target (Organization)** | Cyberdyne Systems |
| **Role** | Engineer |

John
Connor is now linked to Cyberdyne Systems as an **Engineer**.
